title: Agapetes flava
intro: Agapetes flava, commonly known as the golden agapetes, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Agapetes flava include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions and infusions, often used to relieve respiratory symptoms and soothe coughs.
